annotations.label: Logistic function (definition)

anchor.snippet: A logistic function or logistic curve is a common S-shaped curve

annotations.note: Mathlib defines only the standard logistic (sigmoid) `(1 + exp(-x))⁻¹`; the general three-parameter L/k/x₀ logistic is not formalized.

annotations.label: Carrying capacity parameter

anchor.snippet: is the carrying capacity , the supremum of the values of the function

annotations.note: No parametrized logistic is defined in Mathlib, so no `L` (carrying capacity) parameter is present.

annotations.label: Logistic growth rate parameter

anchor.snippet: is the logistic growth rate, the steepness of the curve

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Real.sigmoid` has no `k` parameter for the growth rate.

annotations.label: Midpoint parameter

anchor.snippet: value of the function's midpoint

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Real.sigmoid` has no `x₀` midpoint parameter.

annotations.label: Domain and limits of logistic function

anchor.snippet: The logistic function has domain the real numbers

annotations.note: Limits at ±∞ are proved by `Real.tendsto_sigmoid_atTop` (→ 1) and `Real.tendsto_sigmoid_atBot` (→ 0), with the function defined on all of ℝ.

annotations.label: Standard logistic / sigmoid function

anchor.snippet: is used to define the standard logistic function

annotations.note: `Real.sigmoid x := (1 + exp (-x))⁻¹` is exactly the standard logistic function.

annotations.label: Standard logistic function

anchor.snippet: The standard logistic function is the logistic function with parameters

annotations.note: Same `Real.sigmoid` definition serves as the standard logistic.

annotations.label: Symmetry property of logistic function

anchor.snippet: The logistic function has the symmetry property that

annotations.note: `Real.sigmoid_neg`: `sigmoid (-x) = 1 - sigmoid x` is the Wikipedia symmetry property.

annotations.label: Odd function property

anchor.snippet: is an odd function

annotations.note: Oddness of `sigmoid(x) - 1/2` is not stated as such, but follows immediately from `Real.sigmoid_neg`.

annotations.label: Rotational symmetry about (0, 1/2)

anchor.snippet: The logistic function is thus rotationally symmetrical about the point

annotations.note: The geometric rotational-symmetry statement is not formalized, though `Real.sigmoid_neg` encodes its algebraic content.

annotations.label: Inverse is the logit function

anchor.snippet: The logistic function is the inverse of the natural logit function

annotations.note: The logit function is not defined in Mathlib and no inverse-of-sigmoid statement appears.

annotations.label: Logistic as offset/scaled tanh

anchor.snippet: The logistic function is an offset and scaled hyperbolic tangent function

annotations.note: The identity `sigmoid x = 1/2 + 1/2 · tanh(x/2)` is not proved in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Derivative via tanh form

anchor.snippet: The hyperbolic-tangent relationship leads to another form for the logistic function's derivative

annotations.note: No tanh-based form of the sigmoid derivative is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic angle interpretation

anchor.snippet: the hyperbolic tangent function is the hyperbolic angle on the unit hyperbola

annotations.note: Mathlib has `tanh` but does not formalize the hyperbolic-angle interpretation.

annotations.label: Parametrization of hyperbolas

anchor.snippet: Parametrically, hyperbolic cosine and hyperbolic sine give coordinates on the unit hyperbola

annotations.note: `cosh² - sinh² = 1` is proved, which is the algebraic content of the unit-hyperbola parametrization, but no explicit hyperbola parametrization API exists.

annotations.label: Derivative of standard logistic function

anchor.snippet: The standard logistic function has an easily calculated derivative

annotations.note: `Real.hasDerivAt_sigmoid` gives `deriv sigmoid x = sigmoid x · (1 - sigmoid x)`, matching the Wikipedia formula.

annotations.label: Logistic distribution as location–scale family

anchor.snippet: The logistic distribution is a location–scale family

annotations.note: The logistic distribution is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Antiderivative of logistic function

anchor.snippet: its antiderivative can be computed by the substitution

annotations.note: No antiderivative of the sigmoid (softplus identity) is proved in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Softplus function

anchor.snippet: this is known as the softplus function

annotations.note: A grep for `softplus` in Mathlib returns no hits.

annotations.label: Analyticity of standard logistic function

anchor.snippet: The standard logistic function is analytic on the whole real line

annotations.note: `analyticAt_sigmoid` (and `analyticOnNhd_sigmoid` on `Set.univ`) prove sigmoid is real-analytic everywhere.

annotations.label: n-th derivative formula

anchor.snippet: A formula for the n th derivative of the standard logistic function is

annotations.note: No closed-form n-th derivative of sigmoid is provided in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Taylor series of logistic function

anchor.snippet: therefore its Taylor series about the point

annotations.note: No explicit Taylor series for sigmoid is formalized.

annotations.label: Logistic differential equation

anchor.snippet: The unique standard logistic function is the solution of the simple first-order non-linear ordinary differential equation

annotations.note: The logistic ODE and its characterization of sigmoid is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Phase line / equilibria behavior

anchor.snippet: The qualitative behavior is easily understood in terms of the phase line

annotations.note: Phase-line analysis of the logistic ODE is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bernoulli special case and solution

anchor.snippet: The logistic equation is a special case of the Bernoulli differential equation

annotations.note: The Bernoulli ODE is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Growth behavior of logistic curve

anchor.snippet: the logistic curve shows early exponential growth for negative argument

annotations.note: No exponential-vs-saturation growth statement for the logistic ODE solution is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Generalized differential equation form

anchor.snippet: The differential equation derived above is a special case of a general differential equation

annotations.note: No generalized logistic ODE is formalized.

annotations.label: Probabilistic interpretation of logistic

anchor.snippet: the value of the logistic function is in the range

annotations.note: `unitInterval.sigmoid` and `Real.sigmoid_pos`/`sigmoid_lt_one` show the output lies in (0,1), but no Bernoulli-probability semantics is attached.

annotations.label: Log-odds interpretation

anchor.snippet: In this interpretation the input x is the log-odds for the first alternative

annotations.note: Log-odds / logit is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Softmax function

anchor.snippet: This form immediately generalizes to more alternatives as the softmax function

annotations.note: Grep for `softmax` returns no matches in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Softmax invariance under constant shift

anchor.snippet: the softmax function is invariant under adding a constant to all the logits

annotations.note: Without a softmax definition, the invariance property is also absent.

annotations.label: Generalizations in growth modeling

anchor.snippet: In growth modeling, numerous generalizations exist

annotations.note: No generalized growth-model logistic variants are formalized.

annotations.label: Verhulst population growth equation

anchor.snippet: this model is formalized by the differential equation

annotations.note: Grep for `Verhulst` in Mathlib returns nothing.

annotations.label: Solution of Verhulst equation

anchor.snippet: The solution to the equation

annotations.note: Solution to the Verhulst ODE is not formalized.

annotations.label: Carrying capacity asymptotic behavior

anchor.snippet: The carrying capacity is asymptotically reached independently of the initial value

annotations.note: No asymptotic-to-carrying-capacity statement is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dimensionless form of logistic equation

anchor.snippet: gives the dimensionless differential equation

annotations.note: Dimensionless logistic ODE rescaling is not formalized.

annotations.label: Antiderivative of ecological form

anchor.snippet: The antiderivative of the ecological form of the logistic function can be computed by the substitution

annotations.note: No antiderivative for the ecological-form logistic appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Time-varying carrying capacity model

anchor.snippet: as a consequence it can be time-varying

annotations.note: Time-varying carrying capacity ODE is not formalized.

annotations.label: Periodic carrying capacity yields periodic solution

anchor.snippet: It can be shown

annotations.note: Periodicity of solutions under periodic K(t) is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Logistic delay equation behavior

anchor.snippet: This leads to a logistic delay equation

annotations.note: Delay differential equations are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Logistic regression model

anchor.snippet: Logistic functions are used in logistic regression to model how the probability

annotations.note: Grep for `logistic` reveals no logistic regression formalization.

annotations.label: Rasch model application

anchor.snippet: Another application of the logistic function is in the Rasch model

annotations.note: No Rasch model in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Activation function in neural networks

anchor.snippet: A common choice for the activation or

annotations.note: Neural-network activation functions as such are not formalized; `Real.sigmoid` exists but its use as an activation is not.

annotations.label: Tumor growth model

anchor.section: In medicine: modeling of growth of tumors
anchor.snippet: the logistic differential equation can be used to model the growth of tumors

annotations.note: Tumor growth modeling is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chemotherapy log-kill revision

anchor.section: In medicine: modeling of growth of tumors
anchor.snippet: If a course of chemotherapy is started with a log-kill effect

annotations.note: Log-kill chemotherapy model is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Eradication condition

anchor.section: In medicine: modeling of growth of tumors
anchor.snippet: if the average therapy-induced death rate is greater than the baseline proliferation rate

annotations.note: No eradication-criterion theorem for the chemotherapy model is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Generalized logistic / Richards growth curve for COVID-19

anchor.snippet: also called the Richards growth curve, has been applied to model the early phase

annotations.note: Grep shows no Richards growth curve in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Reduction to logistic and Gompertz

anchor.snippet: The flexibility of the curve

annotations.note: No Gompertz curve or Richards-reduction theorem in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Autocatalytic reaction concentration

anchor.snippet: The concentration of reactants and products in autocatalytic reactions follow the logistic function

annotations.note: Autocatalytic reaction kinetics is not formalized.

annotations.label: Fermi–Dirac distribution

anchor.snippet: The logistic function determines the statistical distribution of fermions over the energy states

annotations.note: Grep for `Fermi` / `FermiDirac` shows no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Mirage modelling

anchor.snippet: The logistic function also finds applications in optics

annotations.note: Mirage modeling is not formalized.

annotations.label: Language change model

anchor.snippet: the logistic function can be used to model language change

annotations.note: Linguistic language-change models are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Crop response S-curve

anchor.snippet: The logistic S-curve can be used for modeling the crop response

annotations.note: Agricultural crop-response models are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Diffusion of innovations

anchor.section: In economics and sociology: diffusion of innovations
anchor.snippet: The logistic function can be used to illustrate the progress of the diffusion of an innovation

annotations.note: Diffusion-of-innovations modeling is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Inflection point determination

anchor.section: Inflection Point Determination in Logistic Growth Regression
anchor.snippet: Logistic growth regressions carry significant uncertainty

annotations.note: Inflection-point estimation for logistic regression is not formalized.

annotations.label: Sequential analysis logistic probability

anchor.snippet: derives the probability of first equaling or exceeding the positive boundary

annotations.note: Sequential analysis / boundary-crossing probability with logistic is not in Mathlib.
